PARANOIA ve sea 
a) We tell the gamemaster that the first time he runs Paranoia he should use the regenerated characters printed in Fold,Out D. Also in all the adventures we publish for use with’ Paranoia, we include pregenerated characters tailored to the adventure. So don’t bbe surprised if your gamemaster hands you a 
regenerated character sheet and asks you to 
play that character Sometimes, however, the gamemaster will tell you to generate your own character. Here's hhow you do so. 
Names 
‘The first thing you need to do is choose a name for your character. In Alpha Complex, ‘names look like this: 
‘Anne-R-KEY 
‘The first part i just a name — in this case, “anne” The second part (the letter) is the character's security clearance, like this R = Red Blue 0 = Orange | = Indigo Y = Yellow V = Violet G = Green = Ultraviolet 
| 3. Character Creation 
Baby production n Alpha Compe s much les mesy and gusting than the fashioned way. 
“infrared” has the same first leter as “In digo," so Infrared characters omit the clearance letter — Fred-LLKRs| is Indigo, but Fred-LKR- is Infrared Next cores a three letter code, representing the name of the sector of Alpha Complex ‘where the character lives. Anne lives in KEY sector. Don't worry about what sector you ‘come from — just pick any three letters you want Last is a number; it's the current clone number. Anne is on clone number 1; she's never been killed. When she is, Anne-R-KE¥2 will take over her job. Now that you know how names work in ‘Alpha Complex, you can make up your own. ‘The custom isto invent some sleazy pun, For instance: ‘Ahmm-R-RUS1 — SowR-DOE Toys-R-USS1 Teach RPETI Vund-R-BAR Howe RYAHA 
Attributes Each character has eight attributes: strength, agility dexterity, endurance, moxie, chutzpah, ‘mechanical aptitude, and power. When you ‘generate a character, you roll the die once for each attribute, and record the number rolled 
fon the character sheet in the “attributes” next to the attribute's name. Ally, the higher your attributes, the better (see page 14 for more detail). ‘Niter you've rolled for your attributes, you can resol two of them. To re-oll, just scratch ‘out your old attribute number, roll the die ‘again, and enter your new roll. If you rolled lower than your old number — 00 bad! You only get two re-olls, and may not reoll twice for the same attribute. Note: If your characters attribute scores total upto less than eighty, he's pretty damn wimpy. itis suggested that you whine and pule until Your gamemaster allows you to roll up a new ‘one (fhe refuses, don’t despair: just get your character killed six times once play begins. This can be done in seconds.) Here's a brief description of the attributes and what they mean: 
Strength measures a character's physical strength. It determines how good a character is at ling, carrying, or ripping open things, or at battering opponents. Strength 20 is Mr. ‘America. Strength 1 i an average desk jockey ‘who played basketball in school. Strength 1 isa sissy with the flu 
Agility measures coordination and balance. Agility determines how good a character is at 
ing and performing actions requiring 
n
